name: Salmon with Miso-Honey Glaze
ingredients: 1 1/4 pounds salmon filet, cut into 4 equal portions
instructions: Preheat grill or broiler.
In a small bowl, combine miso, mirin, honey, and lime juice. Mix to a smooth paste.
Spread 1 tablespoon paste on top of salmon fillet. Grill or broil to desired doneness.
notes: Feel free to add chopped garlic/ginger to the glaze. You can substitute the juice and zest of an orange for the mirin. The glaze is wonderful on pieces of tofu or roasted eggplant, as well.

name: Miso Soup
instructions: In a medium saucepan, cook shallots until translucent in the oil.
Deglaze with vegetable stock or water and bring to a boil. Simmer 5 minutes, add tofu to heat through.
Place miso in a bowl and ladle on about 1/2 cup of broth. Stir to combine then add back into the pot.
Garnish with scallions & drizzle of sesame oil and serve.
notes: Use different types of miso to vary the flavor. For maximum nutrition, miso shouldn't be brought to the boil.

name: Grilled Chicken with Mustard & Basil
ingredients: 4 skinless chicken breast halves, boned and defatted
instructions: In a small bowl, combine mustard, garlic, vinegar, salt, pepper, and basil. Mix well.
Coat chicken breasts with mixture (can marinate for 1 to 3 hours, if desired).
Preheat broiler.
Drizzle roasting pan with oil if desired. Broil chicken breast until cooked through, about 5 minutes on each side. Place chicken on lower rack of the broiler if getting too browned.
notes: Dijon mustard provides flavor as well as tenderizing the chicken breasts. Feel free to pound out the chicken breasts a little to speed up cooking time.

name: Chocolate Yogurt Mousse
instructions: Place chocolate in a medium bowl. Place milk in a medium saucepan. Set chocolate over the pan of milk and melt over medium heat.
Pour hot milk over the chocolate and stir to melt. Add vanilla.
Whisk in yogurt until all is combined. Serve 1/4 cup.
notes: Use this dreamy dessert with some chocolate cake as a base, or a dip for fresh fruits.

name: Bean & Feta Salad with Roasted Vegetables
ingredients: 1 medium red bell pepper, cut into quarters
1 medium tomato, peeled and chopped (about 4 ounces) - for vinaigrette
1 head green leaf lettuce, cleaned and torn into medium pieces
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Lightly coat a sheet pan with olive oil.
Distribute tomatoes, red peppers, and red onions evenly and roast for 15 to 20 minutes or until edges begin to turn brown. Cool and remove skins from tomatoes and peppers. Slice peppers.
To prepare dressing, combine peeled tomato, balsamic vinegar, olive oil and salt in a blender container and puree until smooth.

Arrange 1/4 of lettuce on a large salad plate and sprinkle with 1/4 cup kidney beans. Top with 2 slices roasted tomato, 2 slices roasted onion and 2 slices roasted peppers. Drizzle 2 tablespoons dressing over salad and garnish with 2 tablespoons feta cheese.

name: Chicken Piccata
ingredients: 1 tablespoon capers, packed in vinegar and salt, drained
4 skinless chicken breast halves, boned and defatted
instructions: In a small bowl, combine lemon juice, capers and chicken stock. Set aside.
Place flour in a large bowl. Dredge chicken breast in the flour.

Heat a large sauté pan with oil. Sauté chicken over medium heat until chicken is cooked through and golden brown, about 3 to 5 minutes on each side, or until juices run clear when pierced with a fork.
Add lemon juice mixture to pan and cook until sauce begins to thicken, about 2 minutes.
Serve immediately.
notes: This recipe can easily accommodate the addition of vegetables to the pan: wilt some spinach at the very last moment in the lemony sauce.

name: Cauliflower Fried "Rice"
instructions: Pulse cauliflower in a food processor, or grate it with a box grater into tiny pieces.
Spray a large sauté pan or wok with nonstick vegetable spray. Stir-fry shallots, scallions, carrots, and red onion over medium heat until just tender. Add peas and mix well.
Move vegetables to the side of the pan and pour in eggs. Scramble eggs with turmeric and combine with vegetables.
Add cauliflower rice & basil. Sprinkle tamari over rice and mix gently.
notes: Add whatever vegetables or proteins you like to bulk up this recipe to a full meal.

name: Sesame "Ranch" Dressing
instructions: Combine all ingredients in food processor or blender and puree until smooth.
notes: Nutrient-dense and delicious, savory & tangy dressing is great for crunchy lettuces, kale, or crudites.

name: Maple Pecan-Crusted Chicken
ingredients: Pinch each: salt, cumin, cinnamon, chili powder
4 skinless chicken breast halves, boned and defatted
instructions: Combine syrup, vinegar and shallots in a small saucepan and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to a simmer and cook until liquid has reduced by 1/2. Add stock and bring back to a boil. Reduce to desired consistency.
Preheat oven to 350°. Combine all ingredients for candied pecans. Spread on a parchment-lined baking sheet. Bake 5-10 minutes until browned. Cool and chop.
Increase oven to 450°F. Coat chicken with crust, about 1 tablespoon per breast. Roast about 20 minutes until 165°F. Serve with maple demi-glace and candied pecans.

name: Asian Steak Stir Fry
instructions: Pan roast steak to desired temperature. Let beef rest.
In a hot saute pan add oil and all veggies and saute just enough till veggies start to wilt. Add tofu and finish with soy and a drizzle of sesame oil.
Place 1/2 cup of cooked rice in each of 6 bowls. Slice beef. Divide the beef and vegetable mixture on top. Drizzle hoisin sauce. Garnish with scallions & sesame seeds.
notes: Hoisin Sauce: 90g brown sugar, 45g water, 45g black beans, tamari, ketchup, sherry vinegar, Worcestershire. Bring to a simmer. Keeps 2 weeks.

name: Jammy Sweet Peppers
instructions: Heat sauce pan with olive oil. Add vegetables & sweat over medium heat until soft. Add the rest of the ingredients, lower heat, cover and simmer until jammy texture, about 30 minutes.
Check for seasoning.
notes: Substitute apple, pear, pineapple or peaches, if you prefer.

name: Chicken Parmesan with Roasted Vegetables
instructions: Preheat oven to 450°F convection roast.
Combine oil, garlic, onion, zucchini, tomato, salt, pepper, and vinegar. Toss, transfer to parchment-lined sheet pan and roast about 10 minutes. Cool slightly, top with fresh basil.
Combine ground cereal with herbs, pepper and garlic. Set up breading station with flour and egg.
Pound chicken breasts to 1/4-inch thick. Dredge in flour, egg, then herbed breading. Spray with canola oil.
Bake at 450°F for 10-12 minutes until 165°F. Top with mozzarella and return to oven 1 minute. Serve with vegetable mixture.

name: Roasted Butternut Squash Soup
ingredients: 2 branches fresh thyme, or 1/2 teaspoon dry
instructions: Heat oven to 450°F. Peel, cut, dice and roast butternut squash until tender, about 30 minutes. Reserve.
In a medium saucepan over low heat, sauté onion, garlic, carrots, celery in olive oil. Add wine to deglaze.
Add squash, salt, pepper, thyme, and stock. Cook until soft enough to blend. Blend until smooth.
Add coconut milk and process until well mixed. Garnish with olive oil, pumpkin seeds, and cracked pepper.

name: Matzo Balls (Knaidlach)
instructions: In a large bowl, combine all ingredients except water. Mix well.
Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 1/2 hour.
Bring 4 quarts of water to a boil.
With damp hands shape dough into walnut-sized balls, dropping each into boiling water.
Boil for 1/2 hour. Remove with slotted spoon. Refrigerate until needed. Serve warm.

name: Lentil & Swiss Chard Soup
instructions: Sauté onion, carrot, celery and garlic in oil over medium heat until translucent, about 2-3 minutes.
Add chard, sauté five more minutes. Add water or stock, lentils & potatoes, salt, pepper, red pepper flakes. Simmer until cooked through.
Finish with lemon juice & tamari. Serve with a sprinkle of parsley. Can finish with 1 tablespoon broiled tempeh bacon per serving.
notes: Tempeh Bacon: marinate 3/4 cup tempeh cubes in 2 tablespoons tamari, 1 teaspoon brown sugar, 1/2 teaspoon paprika, dash liquid smoke.

name: Peanut Butter Chocolate Yogurt Smoothie
ingredients: 1/2 tablespoon peanut butter (or sub tahini or almond butter)
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a blender container and mix until smooth.
notes: Substitute 3/4 cup kefir in place of yogurt and milk.

name: Chocolate Almond Butter Delight
instructions: Combine all ingredients in food processor or blender and puree until smooth.
notes: Substitute peanut or any nut butter for the almond butter. Substitute Greek yogurt or Skyr for the ricotta.

name: Chocolate Hazelnut Dessert Hummus
instructions: Combine hummus ingredients in food processor and blend. Taste for seasoning.
Heat oven to 350°F. Cut pitas into wedges. Sprinkle with cinnamon sugar and bake until lightly browned and crispy.
notes: Serve with pita bread toasted with cinnamon sugar, or apple, or pear slices.

name: Beef Tenderloin with Adobo Paste
ingredients: 16 ounces beef tenderloin, cut into 4 equal portions
instructions: Preheat grill or broiler.
In a small bowl, combine brown sugar, lime juice, garlic, and chili powder. Mix to a smooth paste.
Spread 1 teaspoon paste on each side of beef tenderloin fillet. Grill or broil to desired doneness, about 3-5 minutes on each side.
notes: The adobo sauce can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks. It also works on chicken and fish. Contains no salt — adjust to taste.

name: Apple Chia Seed Pudding
instructions: Whisk together almond milk, apple juice, vanilla, and cinnamon until smooth.
Add in chia seeds and whisk together (seeds will settle to bottom — keep whisking every few minutes until they start to expand).
Place in the refrigerator overnight.
Small dice apple to garnish.
notes: Use whatever kind of milk or juice you have on hand. You can even use brewed tea or cold brew coffee. Keeps in the refrigerator for up to 4 days.

name: Stir Fry Sauce
instructions: Combine all ingredients and mix well.
notes: All-purpose stir-fry sauce for any proteins or vegetables. Make a large batch and keep in the fridge for quick meals.

name: Soft Corn Polenta
ingredients: 3 tablespoons soft corn, cut from cob or frozen kernels
instructions: Sauté onion in butter over medium heat until translucent.
Add corn and sauté briefly, about 30 seconds.
Add milk and bring to a boil.
Whisk in polenta, salt and pepper. Cook until thickened, about 3 minutes.
Add cheese and stir until melted. Serve 1/3 cup.
notes: For a creamier consistency, add more milk 1/4 cup at a time.

name: Spicy Peanut & Shrimp Salad
ingredients: 4 egg wontons or egg roll wrappers
instructions: Combine all dressing ingredients in a blender and puree until smooth. Set aside.
Preheat oven to 400°. Cut wontons in half to form triangles. Place on oiled baking sheet.
Bake wontons 8-10 minutes until golden brown.
Combine cabbage, red onion, carrots and broccoli. Toss with dressing.
Divide into 4 servings and top with 2 baked wontons and 4 shrimp.

name: Sourdough Discard Granola
instructions: Heat oven to 275°F.
Combine all ingredients well.
Spread on parchment-lined sheet pans in thin layers.
Toast 40 minutes, stirring every 10 minutes, until crispy and browned.
Leave granola to cool in oven to remove lingering moisture.

name: Beef, Broccolini & Parsley Chimichurri
instructions: Pan roast, broil, or grill steak to desired temperature. Let beef rest.
Combine chimichurri ingredients in a bowl or blender.
Heat oven to 400°F. Roast broccolini with olive oil, salt and pepper, 5-10 minutes.
For pickled shallots: heat all pickling ingredients except shallots to boil, pour over shallots, steep 5 minutes. Keeps 6 weeks refrigerated.

name: Artisan Wheat Bread
instructions: Combine warm water & starter; starter should float if ready.

Add flours & bring dough together, 4-5 minutes of gentle turning. Add salt, mix until just incorporated. Rest 30 minutes, then fold dough (4-5 folds). Repeat folding 2-3 more times at 15-30 minute intervals. Rest 1 hour then shape. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Next day, let bread come to room temp. Heat Dutch oven to 500°F. Bake covered 20 minutes, then uncover and bake another 15-20 minutes.
notes: A classic no-knead sourdough bread. Start it the day before.

name: Feeding the Sourdough Starter
instructions: Mix together and place in a clean container. Leave on counter if using same day; otherwise refrigerate.
Feel free to divide and feed different flours for different breads.
notes: Feed starter each time you use it. If a gray film develops, scrape it off and use what's underneath.

name: Sourdough Discard Einkorn Crackers
ingredients: 4 tablespoons (55g) melted butter (or 3T olive oil for vegan)
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Combine all ingredients and knead to a smooth dough.
Divide into 4 balls. Rest 10-15 minutes covered. Roll out very thin between parchment. Prick with fork, cut into triangles.
Bake 10-12 minutes until nicely browned.

name: Sourdough Chocolate Chip Cookies
instructions: Heat oven to 375°F.
Brown the butter: melt and boil until golden and toasty. Cool to 185g.
Mix flour, baking soda, baking powder.
Whisk melted butter, sugars, vanilla. Whisk in eggs, then starter. Stir in dry mixture briskly for 45 seconds. Add chips. Chill dough 1-2 hours if possible.
Scoop teaspoon-size rounds onto parchment-lined sheets, 1 inch apart. Bake 8-12 minutes until just firm.

name: Neapolitan Sourdough Pizza Crust
ingredients: 500 gm flour (blend or all-purpose or bread flour)
instructions: Mix starter, salt, and water. Add flour and mix until incorporated. Rest 30 minutes.
Stretch and fold: pull dough up and into center, turning vessel. Repeat 8-10 times. Rest 30 minutes. Repeat 3-4 times total.
Bulk ferment at room temperature 4-18 hours until doubled.
Divide into 4 portions, shape into balls, refrigerate 6 hours to 3 days.
Pull dough out before baking. Set oven to 550°F. Shape into 10-inch round. Bake on parchment until blistered, about 5-6 minutes.
notes: Top with classic Margherita (tomato sauce, mozzarella, parmesan) or kale and crème fraîche.

name: Sprouted Multigrain Bread
ingredients: 3/4 cup sprouted grains (quinoa, amaranth, barley, spelt, millet)
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F when ready to bake.
Stir yeast, honey & molasses into warm water. Let sit until foamy, about 5 minutes.
Add cereal & dry ingredients. Knead until smooth and elastic, about 5 minutes. Add sprouted grains and knead until combined.
Let rise 1-2 hours until doubled.
Divide in half, shape into loaves for greased bread pans.
Rise again about 30 minutes until 2 inches above pan rim. Bake 40-45 minutes until 180-200°F. Cool 20 minutes before slicing.

name: Hot Chocolate
ingredients: 1 cup fat free skim milk, or other milk
instructions: In a medium saucepan add chocolate and milk.
Heat milk whisking to blend chocolate with the milk. Let boil up 3 times to make thicker & richer.
notes: Add spices for more comfort: cinnamon, cardamom, chili, nutmeg.

name: Chocolate Truffles
ingredients: 1/2 cup chopped dried cherries (or other dried fruit/nuts)
3 tablespoons cocoa (or toasted coconut, or chopped nuts)
instructions: Heat milk in a small saucepan. Off heat, add chocolate and stir until melted. Mix in cherries. Cool and set (can refrigerate).
Portion into 1-inch round balls. Coat with cocoa. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
notes: The better quality chocolate you use, the better the truffles. Can also roll in chopped nuts or coconut.

name: Chocolate Orange Chia Pudding
instructions: Combine orange juice, almond milk, maple syrup and cocoa powder. Stir until smooth.
Whisk in chia seeds and orange zest until pudding begins to thicken.
Refrigerate overnight.
Garnish with orange segment and shaved dark chocolate.

name: Chocolate Dream Chiffon
ingredients: 1 package organic tofu, or 4 ripe avocados
instructions: In a food processor, soften tofu until creamy. Combine honey, cocoa and vanilla and add to tofu. Blend for 1 minute. Pour into dessert cups and chill.
notes: Swapping 4 avocados for tofu yields a thicker dessert full of healthy fat & fiber (about 157 cal/serving).

name: High Protein Yogurt Pancakes
ingredients: Butter or grapeseed or other neutral oil as needed
instructions: Beat together the cottage cheese, yogurt, and egg yolks. Combine baking soda, flour, salt and sugar. Beat egg whites until fairly stiff but not dry. (Or combine everything in a blender and skip separating the eggs.)

Heat a griddle or large skillet over medium-low heat. Stir flour mixture into cheese mixture, blending well but not beating. Gently fold in beaten egg whites; they should remain somewhat distinct in batter.

Add about 1 tablespoon butter or oil to griddle and coat surface. When hot, add batter by the heaping tablespoon. Cook until lightly browned on bottom, 3 to 5 minutes, then turn and cook second side. Serve immediately.

name: Smoked Chicken Enchiladas
instructions: For enchilada sauce: Wash tomatillos and peppers, place on a sheet pan. Roast at 450°F until nicely browned (about 20 minutes). Remove stems from peppers and blend with tomatillos. Stir in cilantro, salt, and chicken stock.

For smoked chicken: Set smoker to 200-250°F and smoke chicken thighs for 20 minutes. Transfer to a baking pan, toss with roasted green chilies, 1/4 cup enchilada sauce, chicken stock, cumin, and salt. Cover and braise at 350°F for 1 hour. Shred chicken and keep in braising liquid.

For turmeric rice: In a pot over medium heat, cook olive oil and vegetables for 5 minutes. Add rice, cook 5 more minutes. Add turmeric, then chicken stock. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer, cover. Cook until absorbed (about 20 minutes). Salt and fluff.

To assemble: Warm tortillas on a grill or in oven. Place 2 oz smoked chicken and 1/2 oz Oaxaca cheese on each tortilla and roll. Top with enchilada sauce and bake at 350°F until heated through (10-15 minutes). Serve 2 enchiladas with rice, garnished with cabbage and avocado.
notes: Smoking the chicken adds a whole new dimension of flavor. Customize your enchilada sauce — fewer chilies for mild, more tomatillos for tang, skip or add extra cilantro as you like.

name: Roasted Vegetable Salsa
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Lightly spray a baking sheet with canola oil spray.
Halve and core tomatoes and red bell pepper. Peel and halve the onions. Cut jalapeño in half and remove the stem.
Place tomatoes, pepper, onions, and jalapeño on the baking sheet and roast for 30 to 40 minutes or until caramelized. Allow to cool.
Place roasted vegetables, cilantro, black pepper, oregano, salt, vinegar, and lemon juice in a blender and puree.
notes: Deseed jalapeño if you want to make the salsa less spicy.

name: Whole Wheat Pizza Crust
instructions: Add warm water, yeast, sugar, salt, and olive oil in the bowl of a stand mixer with a dough hook attachment. Add flours and mix until the dough separates from the sides of the bowl. Add more flour if too sticky.
Cover the bowl and let rest for 1 hour.
Divide dough into 2 oz pieces, rounding each piece. You can refrigerate (use within a few days) or freeze (use within a month).
When ready to use, place dough on the counter covered, preferably in a warm spot, and allow it to double in size.
Coat the dough in flour or cornmeal and roll out to desired size. Add toppings of choice and cook.
notes: You don't need a fancy pizza oven — just a pizza stone. Heat it in your oven to 500°F, then carefully transfer your pizza onto the stone with a pizza peel.

name: Chicken Bone Broth
instructions: Add chicken bones to a large stock pot and pour in water. Turn heat to medium-high and bring to a boil.
Immediately reduce to a simmer. Maintain a nice gentle simmer.
For the next 4 hours, check your stock intermittently for water level and fat. Cover the pot slightly with a little opening. Add more water as needed to maintain the level.
Remove fat as it accumulates by skimming it off the top with a ladle or spoon.
After 4 hours, add in your washed and roughly chopped vegetables. Return to a simmer and continue watching.
After 8-12 hours total, carefully strain off the bone broth, season with salt, and cool.
notes: A very versatile ingredient — use it as your liquid for making rice or quinoa, or as the base of other soups like Matzo Ball, Chicken Noodle, or Ajiaco. To add more flavor, roast your chicken bones and vegetables before adding them to the stock pot. The darker they are, the more depth of flavor.

name: Carrot Ginger Soup
ingredients: 4 oz sweet potatoes, peeled and diced
instructions: In a large pot over medium-high heat, cook carrots, onions, and sweet potatoes in olive oil until the carrots and sweet potatoes begin to caramelize.
Add minced garlic and ginger and cook for only a minute.
Add vegetable stock, bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer. Continue simmering until carrots and sweet potatoes are soft.
Add contents of the pot to a blender along with thyme, honey, salt, pepper, and purée until smooth and silky.
notes: Carrots are loaded with vitamin A and beta-carotene and are a great source of fiber. Ginger helps fight the flu and common cold, reduces nausea, and aids digestion. You can also use an immersion blender right in the pot.

name: Agua Fresca Sports Drink
instructions: Wash the outside of the watermelon, then cut off the rind. Weigh the remaining fruit and dice (you need 24 oz of usable fruit).
Place the diced watermelon into a blender and purée.
Place 4 cups of hot water into a large pitcher, add evaporated cane sugar and salt. Stir until dissolved.
Using a fine mesh strainer, strain the puréed watermelon into the pitcher.
Add lime juice and stir to combine.
notes: Great for exercise lasting longer than an hour, hot environments, or heavy perspiration. Provides carbohydrates for performance and electrolytes for hydration.

name: Hibiscus Prickly Pear Sports Drink
instructions: Defrost frozen strawberries beforehand.
Place hot water into a large pitcher along with dried hibiscus leaves and steep for 5 minutes.
Strain out the hibiscus leaves, then add the evaporated cane sugar and salt. Stir until dissolved.
Place strawberries in a blender with the lemon juice and purée until smooth. Using a fine mesh strainer, strain into the pitcher of hibiscus tea.
Add the prickly pear purée and stir until combined.
notes: Choose a sports drink when exercising for longer than an hour, in very hot environments, or if your perspiration is salty and heavy.

name: Chocolate Chip Cookies
instructions: In a stand mixer with the paddle attachment, cream together butter, cream cheese, and brown sugar until light and fluffy.
Add egg yolks and vanilla and mix until combined.
In a separate bowl, sift together dry ingredients (flours, baking soda, and salt).
Add dry ingredients and chocolate chips to the stand mixer and mix on low speed, about 10 seconds, until combined.
Portion 3/4 oz cookies onto a parchment-lined baking sheet. Bake at 325°F for 7 minutes. Lightly flatten each cookie, rotate the baking sheet, and bake an additional 3 minutes.
Cool completely before enjoying.
notes: Canyon Ranch’s famous chocolate chip cookies use low-fat cream cheese instead of all butter to lower the fat, plus whole wheat flour for more fiber, protein, and vitamins without extra calories.

name: Oatmeal Cranberry Cookies
instructions: In a stand mixer with the paddle attachment, cream together butter, cream cheese, and brown sugar until light and fluffy.
Add egg yolks and vanilla, mix briefly about 10 seconds until combined.
Combine oat flour, rolled oats, baking soda, cinnamon, salt, and dried cranberries in a bowl.
Add dry ingredients into the stand mixer on low speed until thoroughly mixed.
Portion 3/4 oz cookies onto a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Bake at 325°F for 7 minutes. Lightly flatten each cookie, rotate the pan, and bake an additional 2-3 minutes until golden brown.
Cool completely before enjoying.
notes: These also taste great with raisins or even chocolate chips.

name: Coconut Shrimp Soba Noodles
instructions: Cook soba noodles according to package directions. Drain and rinse under cold water.
Toss noodles with shredded red cabbage, julienned cucumber, and cherry tomatoes.
Heat canola oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Sauté ginger briefly, then add shrimp and red peppers. Cook until shrimp are pink and cooked through.
Add coconut milk and scallions, toss to combine.
Add tofu, season with salt, and gently mix.
Serve noodle mixture topped with shrimp, garnished with roasted peanuts, Thai basil, and mint.

name: Brussels Sprouts and Sweet Potato Hash
instructions: Shred the sweet potato and onion with a box grater.
Add olive oil to a pan over medium-high heat and cook sweet potato and onion until sweet potatoes are tender. Season with salt and pepper.
Add in roasted Brussels sprouts and cook until warmed through.
Split hash between 4 plates, then garnish each plate with toasted almonds and a drizzle of balsamic reduction.

To Make Roasted Brussels Sprouts: Preheat oven to 425°F. Trim the ends of the Brussels sprouts, cut in half. Place onto a sheet pan, drizzle with olive oil, season with salt and pepper. Roast for 20-30 minutes until tender and golden brown.
To Make Toasted Slivered Almonds: Preheat oven to 300°F. Place slivered almonds onto a sheet pan and toast until golden brown (about 10 minutes).
notes: Balsamic reduction is made by simmering balsamic vinegar until reduced by 75% — thick and syrupy. Cook at a bare simmer to avoid burning.

name: Chicken Meatballs with Soy Glaze & Pineapple
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Mix together ground chicken, onion, bell peppers, garlic, sage, apple cider vinegar, salt, pepper, ground ginger, and panko bread crumbs.
Shape into 21 meatballs (about 1-inch) and place in a baking dish large enough to hold them all.
Pour soy glaze over meatballs and bake until cooked through (internal temperature of 165°F, about 18-20 minutes).
Remove from oven and add diced pineapple and chopped scallions.
notes: To make soy glaze: Combine 2 Tbsp canola oil, 2 Tbsp tamari, 1.5 Tbsp honey, 1 Tbsp rice vinegar, 1/2 tsp garlic powder, and 1/4 tsp ground ginger in a blender and purée until smooth.

name: Jerk Chicken
instructions: Place all ingredients, except chicken, in a blender and purée until smooth.
Place chicken breasts in a shallow glass baking dish and pour marinade over the top.
Place in refrigerator and marinate for 20 minutes to 24 hours.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Heat a skillet on medium-high and sear chicken for 1 minute per side.
Finish cooking the chicken in the oven for 15-20 minutes or until it reaches an internal temperature of 165°F.
notes: Marinating overnight gives the best flavor. Adjust habanero to taste for heat level.

name: Berries with Lemon Curd
instructions: Mix lemon zest and evaporated cane sugar in a pot.
Add lemon juice and eggs.
Turn the heat to medium and stir constantly with a rubber spatula until the mixture thickens. Do this quickly so the eggs do not scramble.
Remove from heat and add butter; stir until combined.
Strain curd through cheesecloth and place in a shallow container. Top with plastic wrap and cool in the fridge until set.
To serve: Place 1/2 cup of mixed berries in a bowl and top with 2 oz of lemon curd.

name: Blueberry Muffins
instructions: Add powdered sugar, butter, and low-fat cream cheese to a stand mixer with paddle attachment and blend until light and fluffy. Scrape down the sides of the bowl.
Turn mixer to low speed and add the egg. Mix until fully incorporated, scrape down the bowl.
Combine milk and vanilla and slowly add to the mixer on low speed. Scrape down the bowl.
Fold in dry ingredients: baking soda, baking powder, flours, and salt. Mix on low speed until combined.
Remove the bowl and add blueberries. Gently mix with a spoon to incorporate.
Scoop 2.5 oz of batter into a lightly sprayed muffin pan.
Bake at 375°F for 12-15 minutes, or until a toothpick placed in the center comes out clean.
notes: Requires a stand mixer with paddle attachment.

name: Aguachile Marinade
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a blender and purée.
Allow to sit in the fridge for 15 minutes before straining out solids.
notes: Great as a marinade for shrimp or fish ceviche-style dishes.

name: Artichoke Dip
instructions: Combine cream cheese, sour cream, parmesan, mozzarella, lemon juice, salt, pepper, and chilies in a food processor and mix well.
Add in artichokes and pulse until the artichokes just start to break down. Keep it chunky.
Transfer to a crockpot on high until cheese has melted through. Turn heat down to low and enjoy!

name: No-jito Mocktail
instructions: Place lime juice, simple syrup, and mint leaves into a shaker and shake until combined.
Pour equal portions into 4 serving glasses.
Fill each with ice, then top with sparkling water.
notes: To make simple syrup: Mix equal parts water and evaporated cane sugar. Honey or maple syrup also work as substitutions.

name: Prickly Pear Sunrise Mocktail
instructions: Place prickly pear purée, lemon juice, and orange juice into a shaker. Shake until combined and pour equal portions into 4 glasses.
Fill each glass with ice, then top with sparkling water.
Garnish each glass with an orange wheel.
notes: Prickly pear cactus fruit is available commercially as purée, syrup, frozen, or powder if you can't find fresh.

name: Lemon Herb Roasted Salmon
instructions: Wash herbs and dry with a towel. Chop and set aside.
Place salmon on a sheet pan lined with parchment paper and lightly spray with oil.
Season salmon with olive oil, herbs, salt, and pepper.
Slice lemon into rings and place on top of salmon in a line.
Roast salmon in a 350°F oven for about 25 minutes, or until done.
notes: Salmon is rich in omega-3 fatty acids, which help reduce inflammation and lower blood pressure.

name: Honey Crisp Apple Galette
ingredients: 2 firm apples (Honeycrisp, Granny Smith, or Gala)
1/2 cup heavy cream (for basil whipped cream)
instructions: Place cold butter and flours into a medium bowl. Cut butter into flours with two knives or a pastry cutter. Add salt as you blend.
Add milk and egg yolk, mix with a wooden spoon just until combined and smooth. Do not over mix.
Wrap dough tightly in plastic wrap and refrigerate 30-60 minutes.
Roll dough out to 1/8– 1/4 inch. Cut out 6 circles (4 inches each) and place on a lightly greased baking sheet.
Wash and peel apples, quarter and core them. Slice thinly and toss with remaining filling ingredients.
Arrange seasoned apple slices in the center of each crust, leaving a finger-width border. Roll edges inward. Optionally brush edges with beaten egg and a sprinkle of sugar.
Bake at 350°F for about 15 minutes until crust is golden all over.
For basil whipped cream: Combine heavy cream, minced basil, pinch of salt, and sugar. Whip until stiff peaks form.
notes: You can substitute any favorite fresh fruit or berries as the season changes.

name: Three Sisters Salad
instructions: Combine all vinaigrette ingredients in a blender except canola oil and blend until well mixed.
Slowly drizzle in canola oil.
Toss spinach, arugula, tepary beans, and roasted butternut squash together with 1/3 cup dressing, cotija cheese, and lemon zest.
Coarsely chop pumpkin and sunflower seeds. Mix with chia and hemp seeds.
Evenly divide salad into bowls. Top each serving with 1 tablespoon of super seed mixture.
notes: The sherry shallot vinaigrette makes more than needed — save extra for other salads.

name: Roasted Tomato Vinaigrette
ingredients: 1 medium tomato, peeled and chopped (about 4 oz)
instructions: Peel and chop tomato.
Combine tomato, balsamic vinegar, olive oil, and salt in a blender.
Purée until smooth.
Drizzle over salads or roasted vegetables.
notes: Extracted from the Bean & Feta Salad with Roasted Vegetables recipe. A light, tangy dressing that pairs well with any salad.

name: Thousand Island Dressing
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a blender container and mix well.

name: Alla Checca Sauce
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Cut a shallow X in the tops of the tomatoes. Drop into boiling water for 2 minutes, then transfer to a bowl of ice water. Core and peel tomatoes.
Chop the peeled tomatoes (about 4 cups). Sprinkle with 1/2 tsp salt and mix well. Place in a colander and allow to drain.
Combine garlic, basil, parsley, olive oil, black pepper, and red pepper in a medium saucepan. Add drained tomatoes and heat until warm.
Serve over pasta.
notes: A fresh, no-cook-style Italian sauce — vegan and vegetarian friendly.

name: Apple Cider Vinaigrette
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Refrigerate overnight.
Remove cinnamon sticks before serving.
notes: Fat-free dressing — the apple cider and honey provide all the body and sweetness.

name: Baja Crispy Chicken Tacos
ingredients: 4 skinless chicken breast halves, boned and defatted
instructions: In a blender, combine sour cream, 1 Tbsp lime juice, 1 Tbsp orange juice, 1/2 tsp chili powder, 1/8 tsp salt, and avocado. Purée until smooth. Pour over shredded cabbage and toss well. Set aside.

Combine marinade ingredients (1/4 cup OJ, 1/4 cup lime juice, tequila, olive oil, 1 Tbsp chili powder, 1 tsp salt). Place chicken in a shallow glass baking pan, add marinade. Cover and refrigerate 2 hours.
Preheat grill or broiler. Remove chicken from marinade and grill or broil 15-20 minutes until internal temp reaches 165°F. Discard leftover marinade.
Allow chicken to cool slightly, then chop into small pieces.
Divide chicken among 8 taco shells. Top each with 1 Tbsp shredded cheese and 1 Tbsp Pico de Gallo. Serve 2 tacos with 1 cup cabbage salad.
notes: Includes a creamy avocado cabbage slaw. Marinating for the full 2 hours gives the best flavor.

name: Bran Muffins
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Lightly coat 10 cups of a 12-muffin pan with canola oil spray.
In a medium bowl, combine all dry ingredients.
In a small bowl, combine oil, molasses, honey, egg, and buttermilk and mix well.
Add liquid ingredients to dry ingredients and stir just until moistened. Do not over mix.
Stir in raisins and fill prepared muffin pans ¾ full.
Bake 15-20 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in center comes out clean.
notes: High in fiber — 5g per muffin.

name: Carpaccio of Roasted Beets
instructions: Preheat oven to 450°F. Scrub beets and trim roots and tops. Wrap in foil and bake for 1 hour.
Cool, peel, and thinly slice beets. Divide evenly onto 6 plates. Sprinkle with salt.

Combine water, 1/4 cup diced carrots, shallots, Dijon mustard, red wine vinegar, orange zest, olive oil, salt, and pepper in a blender. Purée until very smooth. Drizzle 2 Tbsp dressing over each serving.
In a small bowl, combine red onion, 2 Tbsp minced carrots, parsley, and chives.
Sprinkle 1 Tbsp of the herb mixture on top of beets. Garnish each serving with 3 orange sections.
notes: Vegan and vegetarian. The carrot-Dijon dressing is the star of this dish.

name: Tortilla-Crusted Chicken with Tomatillo Sauce
ingredients: 4 skinless chicken breast halves, boned and defatted
1 Tbsp toasted and ground cumin seed
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Lightly coat a baking sheet with olive oil spray.
Arrange whole tomatillos on baking sheet and roast for 10 minutes until golden brown. Place in blender with jalapeño, oregano, cilantro, garlic, 1/4 tsp salt, and lime juice. Purée until smooth.
Spread tortilla strips on baking sheet and bake 15 minutes until golden and crisp. Cool, break into pieces, and pulse in a food processor to make fine crumbs.
In a shallow bowl, combine flour, oregano, 2 tsp salt, cayenne, and cumin.
Dip chicken in flour mixture, then in egg, then dredge in tortilla crumbs. Place on a lightly oiled baking pan. Spray lightly with olive oil.
Bake 15-20 minutes until crust is golden and chicken is cooked through. Serve with 1/4 cup tomatillo sauce.
notes: Wear gloves when handling hot peppers. Gluten-free friendly.

name: Tortilla-Crusted Sea Bass with Tomatillo Sauce
ingredients: 1 Tbsp toasted and ground cumin seed
1 lb sea bass fillets, cut into 4 oz portions
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Lightly coat a baking sheet with olive oil.
Arrange whole tomatillos on baking sheet and roast for 10 minutes until golden brown. Place in blender with jalapeño, oregano, cilantro, garlic, 1/4 tsp salt, and lime juice. Purée until smooth.
Spread tortilla strips on baking sheet and bake 15 minutes until golden and crisp. Cool and crush into small pieces.
In a shallow bowl, combine flour, oregano, 2 tsp salt, cayenne, and cumin.
Dip fish in flour mixture, then in egg, then dredge in tortilla crumbs. Place on a lightly oiled baking pan. Spray lightly with olive oil.
Bake 10 minutes until crust is golden and fish flakes easily. Serve with 1/4 cup tomatillo sauce.
notes: Same technique as the chicken version — works beautifully with sea bass. Gluten-free friendly.

name: White Bean Soup
ingredients: 1 cup small white beans, soaked overnight and rinsed
instructions: In a large sauté pan, sauté onions, carrots, celery, and garlic in olive oil until onions begin to turn translucent.

Add beans and stir. Add stock, liquid smoke, basil, oregano, and thyme. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and simmer 45 minutes to 1 hour until beans are soft and beginning to fall apart to thicken the broth.
Once soup begins to thicken, add salt, pepper, Worcestershire sauce, parsley, and lemon juice. Simmer briefly.
notes: Soak beans overnight before cooking. Vegetarian — the liquid smoke adds a subtle smoky depth.

name: Granola
ingredients: 1 Tbsp pineapple or orange juice concentrate
instructions: Preheat oven to 275°F. Lightly coat a baking sheet with canola oil spray.
In a medium bowl, combine oats, oat flour, nuts, cinnamon, and salt. Mix well.
In a small bowl, combine apple juice concentrate, pineapple juice concentrate, coconut milk, brown sugar, vanilla, cashew butter, and maple syrup. Mix well. Add to dry mixture and stir until moist.
Crumble mixture onto baking sheet and bake 45 minutes to 1 hour, stirring after 25 minutes for even cooking.
Remove from oven, break apart while still slightly warm, and toss with dried fruit and heated honey. Cool on baking sheet.
notes: Substitute almond butter or peanut butter for cashew butter. Make oat flour in a food processor from rolled oats.

name: Hoisin Sauce
instructions: In a medium sauté pan, combine oil, garlic, chili flakes, and five-spice powder. Sauté over medium heat until garlic turns golden brown.
Add brown sugar and cook over low heat until sugar caramelizes.
Add remaining ingredients and cook until thick, about 20-30 minutes over low heat.
Cool. Transfer to blender and purée until smooth.
notes: Homemade hoisin — great for stir-fries, glazes, and dipping sauces.

name: Honey Lemon Tarragon Dressing
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a blender container.
Purée until smooth.
Store in refrigerator.
notes: To make yogurt cheese: Place nonfat yogurt in cheesecloth and invert over a bowl in the fridge overnight to drain. 1 quart yogurt = 1 1/2 cups yogurt cheese.

name: Lasagna with Meat Sauce
ingredients: 2 cups fresh marinara sauce (for meat sauce)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Lightly coat a 9x13x2-inch baking pan with canola oil spray.

Brown beef in a large saucepan, then drain. Sauté onions and garlic in olive oil until translucent. Add meat and spices, sauté briefly. Add 2 cups marinara and cook until thickened, about 15-20 minutes.
In a medium bowl, combine ricotta, egg yolks, garlic granules, basil, oregano, salt, and pepper.

Assemble: Spread 1/2 cup marinara in pan. Layer 1.5 pasta sheets, half the ricotta, 1 cup meat sauce, 1/4 cup parmesan. Repeat layers: 1.5 pasta sheets, 1/2 cup marinara, 1 cup meat sauce, 1/4 cup mozzarella. Then 1.5 pasta sheets, remaining ricotta, 1 cup meat sauce, 1/4 cup parmesan. Then 1.5 pasta sheets, 1/2 cup marinara, 1 cup meat sauce, 1/4 cup mozzarella.
Top with remaining pasta sheet, 1/2 cup marinara, 1/4 cup mozzarella, and 1/4 cup parmesan.
Cover with foil and bake 45-50 minutes. Cool slightly and cut into 8 servings.
notes: Uses fat-free ricotta and lean ground beef to keep it lighter. Needs 5 cups total marinara sauce.

name: Lemon Pound Cake
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Lightly coat a 9-inch bundt pan with canola oil spray and dust with flour.
Cream butter, cream cheese, and sugar with an electric mixer on low speed until sugar is dissolved. Add egg yolks and mix until combined.
In a medium bowl, sift together flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
In another bowl, combine buttermilk, lemon extract, lemon peel, and lemon juice.

Add half the flour mixture to creamed mixture and mix on low 30 seconds. Add half the buttermilk mixture and mix until just combined. Repeat with remaining flour and buttermilk. Mix briefly until smooth.
Pour into pan and bake 45 minutes, or until a toothpick comes out clean.
Cool 15 minutes on a wire rack, invert onto a plate, cool completely. Dust with powdered sugar. Slice into 20 servings.
notes: Uses low-fat cream cheese and buttermilk for a lighter pound cake.

name: Lemonade
instructions: Combine sugar and lemon juice in a 2-quart pitcher or jar.
Add water and mix well.
Add ice and serve.
notes: Simple and refreshing — just three ingredients.

name: Manchego Chopped Salad with Shrimp
ingredients: 1 cup diced red or yellow bell pepper
1/2 cup champagne or white wine vinegar
instructions: Make the French vinaigrette: Put garlic in a strainer and submerge in boiling water for 5 seconds until garlic turns white. Place garlic, vinegar, mustard, water, tamari, and pepper in a blender and blend until smooth. Slowly drizzle in oils while blender is running.
In a large bowl, place all diced vegetables and shrimp. Add 1/2 cup dressing and toss until well combined.
Divide salad among 4 plates. Top each with 1 Tbsp shaved Manchego cheese.
notes: Includes a French vinaigrette recipe — makes extra dressing that's great on other salads too.

name: Mustard-Crusted Chicken
instructions: Combine yogurt, mustards, and lemon juice for marinade. Pour over chicken in a glass baking dish. Cover and refrigerate 2 hours or overnight.
Bring chicken stock to a simmer and reduce by half. Set aside to cool.
In a separate pot, melt butter and add flour to form a roux. Cook over low heat 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add cooled stock to hot roux, whisking constantly until mixture boils and thickens.
Add salt, bay leaf, and half the milk. Bring back to a simmer. Make a slurry with remaining milk and cornstarch, add to sauce and simmer until thick. Cook 15 minutes. Remove bay leaf.
Preheat oven to 375°F. Coat marinated chicken in bread crumbs, place in a baking dish. Bake 15-20 minutes until done.
Serve each breast topped with 2 Tbsp sauce.
notes: Marinate overnight for best results. The yogurt-mustard marinade keeps the chicken moist.

name: Ranch Hands BBQ Sauce
instructions: Sauté onions and garlic until translucent.
Add remaining ingredients and simmer for 10 minutes.
Cool and store in refrigerator.
notes: Great on chicken, beef, or pork. The balsamic vinegar adds a nice depth.

name: Roasted Red Pepper and Tomato Bisque
ingredients: 3 red bell peppers, seeded and quartered
1 large red onion, peeled and sliced
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Coat a baking sheet with olive oil spray. Place peppers skin-side up, fill spaces with onion and garlic. Roast 15-20 minutes until pepper skins brown. Run peppers under cold water and remove skins.
In a large saucepan, combine tomatoes, oregano, and chicken stock. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, simmer 1 minute. Add peppers, onions, and garlic.
Cook 10 minutes. Transfer to blender and purée until smooth. Return to saucepan, add basil, sugar, salt, and pepper. Cook briefly. Remove from heat and stir in heavy cream.
Serve 3/4 cup per bowl, garnished with 1 tsp sour cream and 1 Tbsp whole-wheat croutons.

name: Sautéed Shrimp with Roasted Red Pepper Vinaigrette
instructions: Make vinaigrette: Place roasted red peppers, shallots, garlic, basil, rosemary, sugar, and sherry vinegar in a blender. Purée until smooth. With machine running, slowly drizzle in hazelnut oil.

Make saffron orzo risotto: Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil until translucent. Add orzo and stir to coat. Add chicken stock, bring to a boil. Reduce to a simmer, stir constantly until liquid is absorbed and orzo is tender, 10-15 minutes. Stir in saffron, paprika, parsley, and salt.
In a large sauté pan, heat canola oil over medium heat. Add shrimp and sauté until just pink. Season with salt and pepper.
Plate: 2 Tbsp vinaigrette on the bottom, top with 1/3 cup orzo risotto and shrimp.
notes: A composed plate — includes saffron orzo risotto and roasted red pepper vinaigrette.

name: Shrimp Catalan
ingredients: 2 red bell peppers, roasted, seeded, and diced
1 lb peeled and deveined shrimp, tails removed
instructions: In a large sauté pan, sauté shrimp in 2 tsp olive oil until pink. Remove and set aside.
Sauté onions and garlic in remaining olive oil until translucent.
Add wine and increase heat to medium-high. Cook until wine evaporates. Add red peppers and sauté briefly. Remove from heat, cool, and purée in a blender until smooth.
Strain tomatoes and reserve liquid. Combine tomato liquid and saffron in a sauté pan, bring to a simmer. Add tomatoes and pepper purée. Add parsley, cayenne, and salt.
Serve 1 cup over pasta, rice, or grains.
notes: A saffron-infused Spanish-style shrimp dish. Gluten-free.

name: Pico de Gallo
instructions: Place all ingredients in a food processor and mix briefly.
notes: Vegan. Great as a topping for tacos, grilled fish, or with chips.

name: Poblano Roulade
instructions: Preheat broiler. Arrange peppers on baking sheet and broil until charred on all sides, turning occasionally, about 20 minutes.
Place in a stainless steel bowl and cover tightly with plastic wrap. Let stand 10 minutes. Peel chilies and discard skin.
Pull each pepper apart, keeping it in as large a section as possible. Lay between plastic wrap and pound lightly to flatten.
Place 1 1/2 tsp goat cheese, 1 Tbsp diced shrimp, and 1/4 tsp cilantro on each pepper. Sprinkle with salt. Roll up and top each with 1/4 tsp pumpkin seeds.
Bake 8-10 minutes until heated through and seeds are browned.
notes: An elegant appetizer — roasted poblanos stuffed with shrimp and goat cheese.

name: Potato Latkes
ingredients: 4 large russet potatoes, peeled and grated
instructions: In a medium bowl, combine potatoes and onions. Add eggs, salt, pepper, and olive oil. Sift in flour and mix well.
Lightly coat a large sauté pan with canola oil spray. Portion a scant 1/4 cup potato mixture for each latke, flatten, and cook 3 at a time.
Cook until golden brown, about 3 minutes. Flip and finish browning on the other side.
Serve 3 latkes with nonfat yogurt, nonfat sour cream, or applesauce.
notes: Dairy-free. Perfect for Hanukkah or any time.

name: Ranch Dressing
instructions: Bring a pot of water to a boil and add garlic. Reduce to a simmer and cook about 5 minutes. Drain and cool.
In a blender, blend garlic, tamari, Worcestershire, vinegar, sugar, pepper, and salt until smooth.
Thinly slice the green parts of the scallions and add to blender. Pulse only 2-3 times to keep them chunky.
Place sour cream and mayonnaise in a mixing bowl. Pour in blender contents and whisk by hand until incorporated.
notes: Blanching the garlic mellows its bite — skip this step if you prefer a stronger garlic kick. Homemade mayo recommended.

name: Raspberry Lime Smoothie
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a blender and blend until smooth.
notes: High in fiber (8g per serving) with hidden kale for extra nutrition.

name: Red Wine Vinaigrette
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a blender container and purée until smooth.
notes: The anchovies add umami depth without a fishy taste.

name: Salmon Teriyaki
ingredients: 1 lb salmon fillets, cut into 4 equal portions
1/2 cup low-sodium wheat-free tamari soy sauce
instructions: Combine all ingredients except onions and salmon in a blender and process. Stir in onions.
Place salmon in a glass baking dish and pour marinade over it. Marinate 10 minutes to 1 hour, turning occasionally.
Preheat grill or broiler. Grill or broil 3-5 minutes on each side, or until fish flakes easily.
notes: Uses apple juice concentrate for natural sweetness instead of sugar. Wheat-free tamari keeps it gluten-free.

name: Scalloped Potatoes
ingredients: 3 medium russet potatoes, thinly sliced (about 3 cups)
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Lightly coat a 9-inch square baking dish with canola oil.
Place half of the potatoes in the dish and cover with onions. Top with remaining sliced potatoes.
In a blender, combine milk, flour, salt, pepper, and butter. Blend until well mixed. Evenly pour over the potatoes.
Cover and bake for 45 minutes. Remove cover and bake an additional 15 minutes, or until potatoes reach desired crispness on top.
notes: A lightened-up comfort food classic — only 90 calories per serving.

name: Seven Grain Bread
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Lightly coat two medium bread pans with canola oil spray.
Spread pecans and sunflower seeds on a sheet tray and toast until golden brown, about 5 minutes. Set aside.
In a large bowl, combine water, whole-wheat flour, yeast, honey, and flax seed. Let sit in a warm area until doubled in size, about 1 hour.
Add remaining ingredients to yeast mixture and mix well. Knead 3 minutes with a dough hook (or 5 minutes by hand). Dough will be elastic but not smooth.
Place in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and let proof in a warm area for 1 hour. When doubled, punch down and portion into 2 loaves. Place in loaf pans and proof again until doubled.
Bake 30-40 minutes, or until golden brown.
notes: Can also be used for 3 oz sandwich rolls — bake on a sheet pan for 15-20 minutes.

name: Spicy Grilled Shrimp
instructions: In a shallow glass baking pan, combine all ingredients except shrimp. Add shrimp and stir to coat. Marinate uncovered in refrigerator for at least 2 hours.
Pour marinade from shrimp into a small saucepan. Bring to a boil for 1 minute and set aside.
Prepare hot coals for grilling or preheat broiler. Soak skewers in water for 10 minutes.
Thread 3 shrimp onto each skewer. Lightly spray grill with olive oil and baste shrimp with boiled marinade. Grill until pink, about 2 minutes on each side. Do not overcook.
notes: Gluten-free. The balsamic-ketchup marinade doubles as a basting sauce.

name: Strawberry Daiquiri
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a blender and purée until smooth.
notes: A non-alcoholic frozen daiquiri — dairy-free and refreshing.

name: Oatmeal Cookie
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Lightly coat a baking sheet with canola oil spray.
In a large bowl, cream butter, cream cheese, and sugar. Add egg yolk and vanilla extract, mix briefly.
Combine remaining dry ingredients and raisins in a large bowl. Add to creamed mixture and mix until a dough forms.
Portion heaping teaspoonfuls (about 3/4 oz) onto baking sheet 1 1/2 inches apart. Bake 7 minutes. Lightly flatten with finger. Rotate baking sheet and bake 4 more minutes.
notes: Makes a big batch — 38 cookies at only 75 calories each.

name: Orange Vegetable Stir-Fry
instructions: In a medium bowl, combine all sauce ingredients, adding cornstarch last. Stir to dissolve cornstarch and set aside.
Heat wok or sauté pan with canola oil. Add edamame, bell peppers, pineapple, and snap peas. Season with salt and pepper. Stir-fry until vegetables are tender, 3-5 minutes.
Add sauce to the stir-fry and cook until thickened, about 1 minute.
Serve 1 cup stir-fry with 1/2 cup cooked lo mein noodles.
notes: Vegetarian. The orange-ginger sauce ties everything together.

name: Purée of White Bean and Celery Root Soup
ingredients: 1 cup dry white cannellini beans, soaked overnight
instructions: Peel and chop celery root. In a large sauce pot, sauté celery root in olive oil for 5-7 minutes until it starts to brown and caramelize.
Add beans and water. Cook uncovered for 45 minutes, or until beans are soft.
Pour into a blender and purée until smooth. Stir in salt and pepper.
notes: Vegan. Soak beans overnight. The caramelized celery root adds a sweet, earthy depth.

name: Raspberry Chocolate Angel Food Cake
ingredients: 1/2 vanilla bean, seeds scraped from pod
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F. Lightly spray a tube or 9-inch bundt pan with canola oil spray.
Whisk together cocoa powder, flour, 1/3 cup sugar, and salt in a small bowl.
Beat egg whites with cream of tartar on medium-low until frothy. Slowly add 1 cup sugar, almond extract, and vanilla extract. Increase speed to high until firm peaks form.
Sift flour/cocoa mixture over egg whites and fold together by hand.
Pour into pan and bake 40-50 minutes until slightly resistant to touch or a toothpick comes out clean. Cool, loosen sides with a knife, and cut into 20 slices.
Combine raspberries and 4 Tbsp sugar, set aside 15-20 minutes.
Whip cream with 1 Tbsp sugar and vanilla bean seeds until stiff peaks form.
Serve each slice with 1 Tbsp raspberries and 1 Tbsp whipped cream.
notes: A show-stopping dessert at only 145 calories per slice.

name: Red Lentil Soup
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Sauté onions, carrots, celery, and garlic until onions are translucent.
Add vegetable stock, lentils, and herbs. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 1 hour until lentils are soft.
Remove from heat, cool slightly, and purée in a blender until smooth. Add vinegar, Worcestershire, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
notes: No soaking needed — red lentils cook down and thicken naturally.

name: Shrimp Pad Thai
instructions: Combine soy sauce, peanut butter, water, cider vinegar, brown sugar, chili flakes, and coconut milk in a blender. Blend well for the sauce.
Cook rice noodles until just tender. Drain and set aside.
Heat wok and sear shrimp on all sides with sesame oil. Remove and set aside.
Add garlic, ginger juice, carrots, and zucchini to the wok. Sauté until cooked halfway.
Add noodles, shrimp, and sauce. Stir-fry until hot. Divide into 4 portions.
notes: The peanut-coconut sauce comes together in the blender in seconds.

name: Shrimp with Garlic Sauce
ingredients: 1/2 cup peeled, seeded, and diced tomatoes
instructions: In a large sauté pan, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add shrimp and sauté until pink. Do not overcook. Set aside.
In a medium saucepan, combine garlic, shallots, and wine. Simmer until wine is evaporated. Add chicken stock and simmer until reduced by half, about 5 minutes.
Combine cornstarch and water to make a paste. Add to sauce and simmer until thickened, 2-3 minutes.
Remove from heat and add tomatoes, sour cream, heavy cream, parsley, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Serve 1 1/2 Tbsp sauce over shrimp. Garnish with duck prosciutto if desired.
notes: Pairs well with soft corn polenta. The garlic-wine sauce is elegant and light.

name: Sparkling Fruit Sodas
ingredients: 1 1/4 cups fresh fruit (mangoes, berries, or papayas)
6 Tbsp water (for sweet and sour mix)
3 Tbsp simple syrup (for sweet and sour mix)
instructions: Make simple syrup: Mix sugar and water in a small saucepan. Heat until sugar dissolves. Set aside.
Place fruit and 1/4 cup sugar in a blender and purée until smooth.
Combine lime juice, lemon juice, 6 Tbsp water, 3 Tbsp simple syrup, and orange juice for the sweet and sour mix.
In each glass: place 3 Tbsp fruit purée, 2 Tbsp sweet and sour mix, 1 Tbsp simple syrup, and 3/4 cup club soda. Stir, add ice, and serve.
notes: Use any seasonal fruit — mangoes, berries, or papayas all work great.

name: Spring Pea Soup
ingredients: 2 1/2 cups fresh English peas or frozen peas, thawed
instructions: In a large saucepan, sauté onions, leeks, and scallions in olive oil until translucent. Add garlic and peas, sauté 2 minutes.
Add vegetable stock, bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer 30 minutes.
Add chives and mint, cook 5 minutes. Remove from heat, purée in a blender until smooth. Add salt and pepper.
notes: Vegetarian. Fresh mint and chives make this bright and springy.

name: Strawberry Shortcake
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Lightly coat baking sheet with canola oil spray.
Sift together flours, salt, and baking powder. Cut butter and cream cheese into dry ingredients with a pastry cutter. Add buttermilk and mix with a fork until just combined.
Knead gently on a floured surface until dough forms. Roll to 3/4-inch thick. Cut 12 biscuits with a 2 1/2-inch round cutter. Arrange 1 inch apart on baking sheet.
Spray tops lightly with canola oil and bake 10 minutes until golden. Cool.
Combine strawberries, sugar, orange peel, and orange juice. Refrigerate.
Whip cream until stiff peaks form. Chill.
Cut biscuits in half. Place 1/4 cup strawberry mixture on bottom half, cover with top, and garnish with 1 heaping tsp whipped cream.
notes: Uses whole-wheat flour and low-fat cream cheese in the biscuits for a lighter take.

name: Tangy Lime Soy Sauce
instructions: Combine all ingredients and mix well.
notes: No-cook sauce — great for grilled fish, shrimp, or stir-fries.

name: Thai Peanut Sauce
instructions: Place peanut butter in a small bowl. Add salt and sugar, mix until almost dissolved. Add coconut milk, lime juice, ginger juice, rice vinegar, fish sauce, and Tabasco. Mix well to form a thick paste.
In a small saucepan, combine water and tamari and bring to a boil. Whisk in peanut butter mixture. Cook until thickened. Remove from heat.
Serve 2 Tbsp with pasta or as a dipping sauce for chicken kebabs.
notes: A stand mixer works great for blending the paste. Versatile dipping and noodle sauce.

name: Warm Chocolate Cake with Coffee Crème Anglaise
instructions: Lightly coat eight 4-oz ramekins with canola oil spray and dust with confectioners' sugar.
Combine egg yolks with 1 Tbsp sugar. Beat until very pale and thick.
Beat egg whites on high until foamy. Add remaining sugar 1 tsp at a time, beating until stiff peaks form.

Melt chocolate in a double boiler. Remove from heat, stir in yogurt. Temper yolks by adding a small amount of chocolate, then combine. Fold in egg whites. Spoon 1/4 cup into each mold. Refrigerate at least 30 minutes.

For crème anglaise: Combine milk, 1/4 cup sugar, vanilla bean, and coffee in a saucepan. Bring to a simmer. Remove vanilla bean, scrape seeds into mixture. Temper 3 egg yolks with hot milk, add back to pan. Cook 1 minute. Cool, then refrigerate at least 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 325°F. Bake cakes 12-15 minutes until they spring back when touched. Cool 2-3 minutes.
Invert onto plates and serve with 2 Tbsp crème anglaise.
notes: An elegant, individual chocolate cake — only 150 calories. The coffee crème anglaise is the perfect pairing.

name: Watermelon Feta Salad
ingredients: 2 cups seeded and cubed golden watermelon
instructions: In a small bowl, combine vinegar and salt. Stir until salt dissolves.
In a large bowl, toss watermelon and feta cheese with vinegar mixture.
Top with fresh basil.
notes: Gluten-free and vegetarian. Uses both red and golden watermelon for a beautiful presentation.

name: Sesame-Crusted Salmon with Sesame Orange Sauce
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F.
In a shallow bowl, combine sesame seeds and salt. Sprinkle over salmon.
In a large sauté pan, heat sesame oil over medium heat. Sauté salmon 1 minute on each side, then place in oven for 5-10 minutes.
For sauce: Combine sweet chili sauce, orange juice, Worcestershire, rice vinegar, and toasted sesame oil.
Serve sauce atop salmon.
notes: The sesame orange sauce is a no-cook blend — comes together in seconds.

name: Roasted Fennel
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Cut fennel bulb into quarters. Season with salt and pepper.
Place on baking pan and roast about 20 minutes until fennel begins to brown.
Add 1/2 cup water to pan and cover with aluminum foil. Return to oven and roast until soft, about 20 more minutes.
notes: Vegetarian. Only 20 calories — a simple, elegant side dish. Just 3 ingredients.

name: Roasted Corn Chowder
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Lightly coat a sheet pan with canola oil spray. Spread corn evenly and roast 5-10 minutes.
In a large saucepan, heat canola oil over medium heat. Add roasted corn, garlic, onions, jalapeño, and green chilies. Cook until onions are translucent.
Add cilantro and oregano, sauté briefly. Add dry herbs and spices, sauté 30 more seconds.
Add water and cream corn. Cook over medium-low heat 30-45 minutes. Cool. Purée in a blender until smooth.
notes: Vegetarian. Choose cream corn without added fat or preservatives.

name: Roasted Chicken with Gravy
ingredients: 1/4 tsp freshly ground black pepper (for potatoes)
pinch freshly ground black pepper (for gravy)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Mix celery, carrots, and onions. Stuff chicken with vegetable mixture. Season with salt and pepper.
Place in a roasting pan, cover, and roast 1-1.5 hours until juices run clear. Cut in half, remove back, ribs, wings, and drumstick. Separate thigh and breast, remove skin.
For mashed potatoes: Peel and cube potatoes. Boil 10-15 minutes until tender. Drain. Add milk, garlic, chives, salt, pepper, and butter. Whip until smooth.
For gravy: Sauté onions in canola oil and butter until translucent. Add flour, cook 3-5 minutes. Add broth and whisk until it boils and thickens. Add pepper and sage. Strain.
Serve 4 oz chicken with 1/3 cup mashed potatoes and 1/4 cup gravy.
notes: A complete comfort food dinner — includes garlic mashed potatoes and sage gravy.

name: Red Chili Chicken Quesadilla
ingredients: 1/2 cup shredded low-fat Monterey Jack cheese
1/2 cup roasted poblano peppers, skinned, seeded, and diced
3 boneless, skinless chicken breasts (4 oz each), diced
instructions: In a large sauté pan over medium heat, sauté onions in canola oil until translucent. Add diced chicken and cook through.
Add salt, pepper, garlic granules, cumin, chili powder, chicken stock, and bay leaf. Cook until liquid is reduced by half. Add flour and cook until thickened. Remove bay leaf.
Spoon 1/2 cup red chili chicken on one half of each tortilla. Spread 2 Tbsp cheese and 2 Tbsp roasted peppers over chicken. Fold in half.
Place on hot grill pan or under broiler, turning once, until cheese is melted.
Cut each quesadilla into quarters. Serve with 1 cup mixed greens.
notes: Whole-wheat tortillas and low-fat cheese keep it lighter. The red chili chicken filling is hearty and flavorful.

name: Canyon Ranch Spa Bread
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Spray an 8 1/2 by 4 1/2 inch loaf pan with cooking spray.
In a mixing bowl, combine flour, bran, baking powder, baking soda, and cinnamon. Stir until very well mixed.
Add the raisins and mix again.
In another bowl, whisk together the buttermilk, egg, and vanilla.
Pour the liquid ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until fully blended.
Transfer mixture to prepared loaf pan. If using nuts, sprinkle them across the top.
Bake for 45 to 50 minutes, or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean.
Place the bread, while still in the pan, on its side on a wire rack to cool.
notes: Lightly sweet cinnamon and vanilla flavored quick bread. Can substitute all-purpose flour for white whole wheat. Oat fiber can be used in place of wheat bran.

name: Toasted Coriander & Carrot Soup
ingredients: 2 cups chopped yellow or white onion
4 cups peeled carrots, cut into 1/2-inch chunks
instructions: In a large heavy-bottomed pot, combine the oil and coriander seeds. Toast over medium heat, stirring frequently, until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the onion and garlic, season with 1 teaspoon of salt and a few cracks of pepper, and cook until onions are translucent, 4 to 5 minutes.
Add the carrots, remaining teaspoon of salt, and pepper. Stir to coat. Stir in the butter and toss until completely melted.
Add the brown sugar and stir until it dissolves. Pour in the stock, bring to a simmer, and cook until carrots are just tender, 8 to 10 minutes.
Remove from heat. Working in batches if necessary, carefully transfer to a blender and blend until smooth.
Strain through a fine-mesh strainer back into the pot. Taste for seasoning. Serve hot.
notes: Recipe from The Lost Kitchen Volume 2. Try garnishing with a drizzle of honey, a dollop of crème fraîche or yogurt, sunflower seeds, or fresh cilantro.

name: Fresh Celery Soup
ingredients: 4 cups chopped celery (about 1 bunch), leaves reserved for garnish
1 cup peeled russet potatoes, cut into 2-inch pieces
instructions: Toast celery seeds in a dry heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat, stirring frequently, until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add olive oil and stir.
Add celery, onion, carrot, and garlic. Season with celery salt and pepper. Cook, stirring, until onions are translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add potatoes, season with kosher salt and pepper, and stir well. Add stock and bring to a slow boil.
Reduce heat to low and cook, stirring occasionally, until potatoes are tender, about 30 minutes.
Ladle half the soup into a blender with half the butter. Blend until smooth and return to pot. Repeat with remaining soup and butter.
Stir in cream. Taste for seasoning. Serve garnished with reserved celery leaves.
notes: Recipe from Erin French. Fresh, sweet celery cooked down with aromatics and given decadent body with butter and cream.

name: Popovers with Honey Butter
ingredients: 8 tablespoons (1 stick) unsalted butter, room temperature
instructions: Make honey butter: Beat butter until light and fluffy, 3-5 minutes. Drizzle in half the honey and beat to combine. Transfer to a bowl, drizzle remaining honey on top, sprinkle with flaky salt.
Make popovers: Gently warm milk until just lukewarm. In a blender, combine warm milk, eggs, powdered sugar, and salt. Blend until smooth.
Add melted butter and blend, then add flour and blend until batter is smooth and frothy. Let rest 15 minutes.
Preheat oven to 450°F. Place empty popover pan in oven to heat for 5 minutes.
Remove hot pan and brush or spray each cup with oil. Divide batter evenly, filling cups about three-quarters full.
Bake 20 minutes without opening the door. Reduce heat to 350°F and bake 8-10 more minutes until deep golden brown.
For drier popovers, pierce tops with a knife to release steam and return to oven for 1 minute. Serve immediately with honey butter.
notes: Recipe from Erin French. Not-too-sweet baked eggy puffs — perfect for Sundays, holidays, and everyday. Serve with honey butter or butter and jam.

name: Glazed Meatloaf
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F.
Combine ground meat, carrots, onion, cheese, bread cubes, thyme, egg, milk, salt, and pepper in a large bowl. Mix with your hands until very well combined.
Pack mixture tightly into a 9x5-inch loaf pan.
Make the glaze: Whisk together brown sugar, ketchup, and Dijon mustard until well combined.
Brush a thick coat of glaze on top of the meatloaf.
Bake until bubbly around edges and internal temp reaches 150°F, about 45 minutes.
Let rest 10-15 minutes, then remove from pan, slice, and serve.
notes: Recipe from Erin French. A comfort classic with pecorino cheese and sourdough bread cubes mixed in, topped with a sweet and tangy glaze.

name: Triple Berry Yogurt
instructions: Add yogurt, berries, and milk to a blender (such as a NutriBullet).
Blend until silky smooth.
Serve immediately.
notes: Use frozen berries like Wyman's Triple Berry blend for convenience and to maximize fiber. Raspberries, blackberries, and blueberries are the highest-fiber berry options.

name: Lemon-Parmesan Broccoli
instructions: Bring a pot of salted water to a boil. Add broccoli florets and cook until bright green and just tender, about 3 to 4 minutes. Drain well.
Toss hot broccoli with butter until melted and evenly coated.
Add lemon juice and lemon zest. Toss to combine.
Transfer to a serving dish, sprinkle with grated Parmesan and a pinch of salt. Serve immediately.
notes: Steaming works just as well — steam for 4 to 5 minutes. Don't overcook; the broccoli should still have a slight bite. Double the recipe easily for meal prep.

name: Roasted Cauliflower with Lemon & Parmesan
ingredients: 1 large head cauliflower, broken into bite-sized florets
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Break cauliflower into bite-sized florets. Toss with olive oil, salt, and several grinds of pepper. Spread evenly onto a rimmed baking sheet.
Roast 25 to 35 minutes, or until tender and nicely browned around the edges.
Remove from oven, toss with lemon zest, Parmesan, and parsley if using. Season to taste and serve.
notes: The key is high heat and not overcrowding the pan — give the florets room so they roast rather than steam. Recipe adapted from Love and Lemons.

name: Roasted Broccolini & Lemon with Parmesan
ingredients: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
½ cup finely grated Parmesan cheese
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Thinly slice half the lemon into rounds. Set the other half aside.
On a rimmed baking sheet, toss the lemon slices, smashed garlic, and broccolini with the olive oil. Season with salt and pepper.
Sprinkle evenly with the Parmesan cheese.
Roast until the broccolini is bright green and starting to char and the cheese is golden brown, 10 to 15 minutes.
Remove from oven, squeeze the reserved lemon half over the top, and serve.
notes: The charred lemon slices become almost candy-like and are meant to be eaten. If you can't find broccolini, use 1 pound regular broccoli cut into long thin spears.

name: Roasted Brussels Sprouts
ingredients: 1½ pounds Brussels sprouts, trimmed and halved through the core
½ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Trim and halve the Brussels sprouts. Remove any yellow outer leaves.
In a large bowl, toss the Brussels sprouts with olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Spread in a single layer on a sheet pan.
Roast 35 to 40 minutes, until crisp and browned on the outside and tender on the inside. Toss once during roasting.
Taste for seasoning and serve hot.
notes: The key is high heat and a single layer — don't overcrowd or they'll steam instead of roast. Some loose leaves will get extra crispy, which is a bonus. For a variation, try Ina's balsamic version: drizzle with balsamic vinegar and add diced pancetta.

name: House-Made Italian Dressing
ingredients: 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard, no sugar added
instructions: Combine all ingredients together in a blender and blend well to combine.
Transfer the dressing to a storage container and keep in the refrigerator for up to 2 months.
Note: it is best to let this sit for 24 hours before using for the first time.
notes: This keeps for up to 2 months refrigerated — make a big batch. The overnight rest allows the herbs to fully infuse.

name: Honey Mustard Vinaigrette
instructions: Place the whole-grain mustard, honey, vinegar, Dijon mustard, shallots, and garlic in the bowl of a blender and process on high speed until well combined, about 30 seconds.
Add the remaining ingredients and process on low speed until smooth, 30 to 45 seconds.
Use immediately or transfer to an airtight container and refrigerate until ready to use.
notes: Keeps refrigerated for up to four days. The combination of whole-grain and Dijon mustard gives it both texture and punch.

name: Fine-Herb Vinaigrette
ingredients: ¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
instructions: Place the stock, basil, cilantro, oregano, parsley, mustard, honey, garlic, shallots, and rosemary in the bowl of a blender and process on high speed until well blended, about 30 seconds.
Add the remaining ingredients and process on high speed until smooth, 20 to 30 seconds.
Use immediately or transfer to an airtight container and refrigerate until ready to use.
notes: Only 30 calories per serving — the vegetable stock base keeps it incredibly light. Keeps refrigerated for up to four days. Use the freshest herbs you can find.

name: Ranch Dressing
ingredients: ⅛ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
instructions: Combine the mayonnaise, green onions, roasted garlic, herbs, and fresh garlic in the bowl of a blender and process on high speed until well combined, 30 to 45 seconds.
Add the remaining ingredients and process on low speed until smooth, about 30 seconds.
Use immediately or transfer to an airtight container and refrigerate until ready to use.
notes: Keeps refrigerated for up to seven days. The combination of roasted and fresh garlic gives it depth.

name: Balsamic Vinaigrette
ingredients: ⅔ cup balsamic vinegar (or red wine vinegar or sherry vinegar)
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a blender and emulsify until smooth.
notes: Swap the vinegar to change the character entirely: balsamic for sweetness, red wine for sharpness, sherry for nuttiness. All three work beautifully.

name: Eggless Caesar Dressing
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a blender and emulsify until smooth and creamy.
notes: No raw egg, no anchovy — just clean, bright Caesar flavor from Parmesan, garlic, and lemon.

name: Avocado Poblano Dressing
instructions: Roast the poblanos over an open flame or under the broiler until charred on all sides. Place in a bowl covered with plastic wrap to steam for 10 minutes. Peel, deseed, and roughly chop.
Add poblanos and all remaining ingredients to a blender. Puree until smooth.
Adjust seasoning with additional salt and lime juice as needed.
notes: The roasted poblano adds a gentle smoky heat. Great as a dressing, dip for veggies, or drizzle over grain bowls.

name: White Soy Vinaigrette
ingredients: ½ teaspoon yuzu juice (or lime juice)
instructions: Lightly toast the sesame seeds in a dry pan until golden and fragrant.
Add all ingredients to a blender and emulsify until smooth, making sure the sesame seeds are fully pureed.
notes: A uniquely creamy, nutty dressing with Japanese-inspired flavors. Yuzu juice is ideal but lime is a fine substitute.

name: Sesame Ginger Dressing
ingredients: 1 tablespoon fresh ginger, peeled and grated
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a blender and emulsify until smooth.
notes: Perfect on Asian-inspired salads, grain bowls, or as a marinade for chicken or tofu.

name: Lemonette
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a blender and emulsify until smooth.
notes: Beautifully simple — just three ingredients. The lemon-forward brightness works on everything from greens to grilled fish.

name: Lime Yogurt Sauce
instructions: Add yogurt to a bowl. Zest the lime over the yogurt using a microplane.
Cut the lime in half and squeeze the juice in, making sure no seeds get through.
Whisk together until smooth. Pour into a squeeze bottle for easy use.
notes: Only 25 calories per serving — a tangy, probiotic-rich alternative to heavier dressings. Great on tacos, grain bowls, or as a dip.

name: Cilantro Lime Vinaigrette
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a blender and emulsify until smooth and vibrant green.
notes: Bright and herbaceous — pairs perfectly with Southwest or Mexican-inspired salads.

name: Ottolenghi's Basic Hummus
instructions: Soak chickpeas overnight in a large bowl covered with plenty of cold water.
Drain the chickpeas. In a medium saucepan over high heat, cook the drained chickpeas with the baking soda for about 3 minutes, stirring constantly. This roughens the skins and helps them cook faster.
Add the water, bring to a boil, and cook 20 to 40 minutes, skimming off any foam and loose skins, until the chickpeas are very soft and falling apart.

Drain the chickpeas, reserving some cooking liquid. Place chickpeas in a food processor with the tahini, lemon juice, garlic, and a big pinch of salt. Process until very smooth and creamy, adding a few tablespoons of cooking liquid if needed to loosen.
Cover and rest at least 30 minutes before serving. Drizzle generously with olive oil.
notes: The secret is cooking chickpeas with baking soda — it breaks down the skins so the hummus gets impossibly smooth without peeling each one. Keeps 3 days refrigerated.

name: World's Best Chocolate Cake
ingredients: 1 cup plus 2 tablespoons (250g) unsalted butter, cubed, plus extra for pan
1½ teaspoons instant coffee dissolved in 1½ cups boiling water
¼ cup (30g) Dutch-process cocoa powder, plus extra for dusting
For ganache (optional): 7 oz dark chocolate, ¾ cup heavy cream, 1 tablespoon golden syrup, 1 tablespoon butter
instructions: Preheat oven to 340°F. Butter and line a 9-inch round cake pan.
Place the butter, chocolate, and hot coffee in a large heatproof bowl. Stir until melted and smooth.
Whisk in the sugar until dissolved, then add eggs and vanilla and whisk until smooth.
Sift in the flour, cocoa powder, and salt. Whisk until just combined — no lumps, but don't overwork it.
Pour into the prepared pan and bake about 1 hour, until a skewer comes out with just a few moist crumbs.
Cool completely in the pan. For the ganache: heat cream, pour over chopped chocolate, stir until smooth, add syrup and butter. Pour over cooled cake.
notes: The easiest impressive cake you'll ever make — everything goes into one bowl. The coffee deepens the chocolate flavor without making it taste like coffee. From Ottolenghi and Helen Goh's cookbook Sweet.

name: Charred Cherry Tomatoes with Cold Yoghurt
ingredients: 3 sprigs fresh oregano, plus picked leaves for garnish
3 strips lemon peel plus 1 teaspoon lemon zest
1 teaspoon Urfa chili flakes (or ½ teaspoon regular chili flakes)
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (fan) or 450°F (conventional).
Toss the cherry tomatoes with olive oil, cumin seeds, sugar, garlic, thyme, oregano sprigs, lemon strips, ½ teaspoon salt, and a good grind of pepper. Spread on a baking sheet.
Roast 10 minutes until blistering and bubbling, then switch to broil and grill 5 to 7 minutes until the tomatoes start to blacken on top.
Meanwhile, mix the yoghurt with the lemon zest and a pinch of salt. Keep cold in the fridge.

Spread the cold yoghurt on a shallow platter, making a well with the back of a spoon. Spoon the hot tomatoes and all their juices, garlic, and herbs over the top. Finish with oregano leaves and chili flakes. Serve immediately with bread.
notes: The magic is the contrast: blistering hot, caramelized tomatoes hitting ice-cold tangy yoghurt. From Ottolenghi Simple. Serve with crusty bread to soak up the juices.

name: Grilled Chicken with Mint Dressing & Corn Salad
instructions: Preheat the grill to high.
Add all of the dressing ingredients to a blender and blend until silky smooth.
Season the chicken generously with salt and pepper and brush the corn with 1 tablespoon of olive oil.

Grill the corn until charred on all sides, then remove from the grill and set aside. Grill the chicken for about 3 minutes on each side, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 165°F. Timing depends on how thin your cutlets are.
Cut the kernels off the corn. Mix together the remaining salad ingredients and fold in half the dressing. Plate the chicken, then top with the remaining dressing and salad. Enjoy!
notes: A bright, summery grilled chicken salad. The mint dressing doubles as a marinade and a finishing sauce. Add quinoa to make it a full grain bowl, or avocado for extra richness — both optional. Nutrition is estimated for the base recipe (excludes optional quinoa & avocado).

name: Morning Glory Muffins
ingredients: 1/3 cup vegetable oil or melted coconut oil
1/3 cup orange juice or pineapple juice
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Spray a 12-count muffin pan with nonstick spray or use greased cupcake liners. Prepare a second pan or bake in batches.
