Whisk flour, baking soda, cinnamon, ginger, salt, and flaxseed in a large bowl. In a medium bowl, whisk eggs, brown sugar, honey, oil, applesauce, orange zest, orange juice, and vanilla until combined. Stir in carrots and apples. Pour wet into dry, add raisins and pecans, fold until just combined.
Fill liners to the top. Bake 5 minutes at 425°F, then reduce to 350°F and bake 18 more minutes until a toothpick comes out clean. Cool 10 minutes in pan, then transfer to wire rack.
Store at room temperature for 2 days or refrigerate up to 1 week.
notes: The high initial oven temperature creates a nice domed top. Do not overmix the batter.

name: Sheet-Pan Baked Feta with Broccolini, Tomatoes & Lemon
ingredients: 1 bunch broccolini, ends trimmed, thick stalks split lengthwise (or broccoli, cut into bite-size pieces)
1 small red onion, peeled, quartered and cut into 2-inch wedges
1 lemon, 1/2 cut into thin rounds and the remaining 1/2 left intact, for serving
3 tablespoons olive oil, plus more for serving
2 (6- to 8-ounce) blocks feta, cut into 1-inch slices
Cooked orzo or farro, for serving
1/2 cup fresh basil or cilantro leaves and fine stems, roughly chopped (optional)
instructions: Heat the oven to 400°F with a rack set in the lower third. On a sheet pan, combine the broccolini, tomatoes, onion and lemon slices with the olive oil and toss. Add cumin and red-pepper flakes, season with salt and pepper, and toss again until evenly coated. Nestle the feta slices into the vegetables (it's OK if they break apart a little).

Roast 15 to 20 minutes, stirring halfway through but leaving the feta in place, until the broccolini is charred at the tips, the stems are easily pierced with a fork and the tomato skins start to blister and break down.
Serve over orzo or farro. Drizzle with olive oil and serve with the remaining lemon half for squeezing. Top with fresh herbs, if using.
notes: By Yasmin Fahr for The New York Times. Baked feta turns almost creamy, like goat cheese but with feta's tang. Only the bottom 1/2-inch of broccolini stalks needs trimming. Nutrition values are estimated (excludes orzo/farro).

name: Turkey & Zucchini Burgers with Green Onion & Cumin
ingredients: about 6 1/2 tablespoons sunflower oil, for searing
instructions: Make the sauce: place all sauce ingredients in a small bowl, stir well, and set aside or chill until needed.
Preheat oven to 425°F. In a large bowl, combine all the burger ingredients except the sunflower oil. Mix with your hands and shape into about 18 burgers, each about 1 1/2 oz.

Pour enough sunflower oil into a large frying pan to form a layer about 1/8-inch thick. Heat over medium heat until hot, then sear the burgers in batches on all sides, about 4 minutes per batch, adding oil as needed, until golden brown.

Carefully transfer the seared burgers to a baking sheet lined with waxed paper and bake 5 to 7 minutes, until just cooked through. Serve warm or at room temperature with the sauce spooned over or on the side.
notes: From Ottolenghi's Jerusalem. The creamy sumac sauce is fantastically sharp and pairs well with most non-red meats and grilled vegetables. The sauce can be made ahead; after a day the flavors mellow, so reinvigorate it with extra sumac and lemon juice. The burgers are very portable — great cold from the fridge or packed in a lunchbox. Nutrition is estimated (includes sauce).

name: Lime & Poppy Seed Slaw with Curry Leaf Oil
ingredients: 1 white cabbage (about 1 lb 14 oz), core removed and head finely sliced
1 red onion, halved and very thinly sliced
1 small Fresno chile, thinly sliced (seeds and all)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a medium baking sheet with parchment paper.

Make the turmeric cashews: put the sugar, oil, turmeric, and 2 tablespoons water into a small saucepan. Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring often, then add the cashews and cumin. Cook 3-4 minutes, stirring constantly, until the nuts are coated in a sticky glaze. Transfer to the baking sheet, spreading the nuts out, and bake 14 minutes, stirring once halfway, until golden. Set aside to cool completely.

Make the curry leaf oil: put the chile and oil into a small frying pan over medium heat and cook 7 minutes, until the chile starts to develop a shine. Add the curry leaves and cook 2-3 minutes more, stirring often, until the leaves turn translucent. Transfer to a bowl and set aside.
Make the dressing: whisk the lime juice, mustard, garlic, poppy seeds, and 1/4 teaspoon salt in a small bowl. Slowly drizzle in the olive oil, whisking continuously, until incorporated.

In a large bowl, combine the cabbage, carrots, onion, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and a good grind of pepper. Pour in the dressing, mixing well, and let soften slightly, about 15 minutes. Fold in the cilantro and mint, then transfer to a large serving platter. Drizzle the curry leaf oil all over and sprinkle with a handful of the turmeric cashews, serving the rest in a bowl alongside.
notes: From Ottolenghi Test Kitchen. A zingy slaw — eat al fresco or on its own. Turmeric cashews keep up to a week in a sealed container; consider doubling them. Play with the nuts (peanuts or almonds work too) and other crunchy veg like kohlrabi. No curry leaves? Leave them out and stir fresh herbs into the oil instead. Nutrition is estimated.

name: Skillet-Roasted Chicken with Whole Lemon
ingredients: 2 teaspoons fresh thyme leaves (or 1/2 teaspoon dried thyme)
1/2 cup dry white wine, such as Pinot Grigio
instructions: Preheat oven to 450°F.

Place the thyme, fennel seeds, 1 tablespoon salt, and 1 teaspoon pepper in a mini food processor or spice grinder and process until ground. Pour the olive oil into a small measuring cup, stir in the herb mixture, and set aside.

Distribute the lemon slices in a 12-inch oven-safe skillet and distribute the onion and garlic on top. Place the chicken skin side down on top of the onion and brush with half the oil-herb mixture. Turn the chicken skin side up, pat it dry with paper towel (very important!), and brush all over with the rest.

Roast the chicken 30 minutes. Pour the wine into the pan (not on the chicken!) and roast another 10-15 minutes, until a meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the breast registers at least 155°F.
Remove from the oven, sprinkle with the lemon juice, cover the skillet with foil, and rest 10-15 minutes. Cut the chicken and serve with the pan juices, cooked lemon, and onion.
notes: One of my most favorite chicken dishes — a double pop of lemon, with a whole lemon roasted alongside the chicken and fresh lemon juice added after cooking. Don't be intimidated by butterflying the chicken; you can ask a butcher to do it, or do it yourself (watch a YouTube video first). To slice the lemon: trim the ends, cut in half through the stem, then thinly slice crosswise. Nutrition is estimated.

name: Kale and Quinoa Salad with Ricotta Salata
ingredients: 8 ounces black kale (Cavolo Nero / Lacinato / Dinosaur / Tuscan)
1/2 cup slivered almonds, very well toasted and cooled
1/3 cup dried cherries, chopped a bit
2 ounces ricotta salata, crumbled or finely grated
Few gratings of fresh lemon zest
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Rinse the quinoa well in a small colander (essential to remove bitterness). Place quinoa and 1 1/2 cups water in a small saucepan with a couple pinches of salt and bring to a simmer. Simmer at a very low temperature 15-20 minutes, until tender. Drain any un-absorbed liquid and spread the quinoa on a plate to cool quickly.

Wash the kale and dry it well. With a knife, remove the rib from each stalk, leaving long strips of leaves. Stack the leaves in small batches, roll them tightly the long way, and cut crosswise into thin ribbons. Add the kale ribbons to a large salad bowl, then add the remaining salad ingredients and toss to mix.
Whisk the dressing ingredients together in a small dish and pour over the salad. Season with salt and pepper to taste, then dig in.
notes: Adapted from The Smitten Kitchen Cookbook and The Smith. The salad wilts a bit and seems smaller the longer it sits with the dressing, so it holds well. Nutrition is estimated (per side serving).

name: Linguine with Shrimp Scampi
ingredients: 2 pounds large shrimp (about 32), peeled and deveined
1/2 cup freshly squeezed lemon juice (4 lemons)
instructions: Drizzle some oil into a large pot of boiling salted water, add 1 tablespoon of salt and the linguine, and cook 7 to 10 minutes, or according to package directions.

Meanwhile, in another large (12-inch) heavy-bottomed pan, melt the butter and olive oil over medium-low heat. Add the garlic and sauté 1 minute — be careful, the garlic burns easily. Add the shrimp, 1 tablespoon salt, and the pepper, and sauté until the shrimp have just turned pink, about 5 minutes, stirring often. Remove from the heat, add the parsley, lemon zest, lemon juice, lemon slices, and red pepper flakes, and toss to combine.
When the pasta is done, drain the cooked linguine and return it to the pot. Immediately add the shrimp and sauce, toss well, and serve.
notes: From Ina Garten (Barefoot Contessa), originally written for her Martha Stewart Living column. A quick meal to pull together — buy peeled and deveined shrimp to make it even faster. Nutrition is estimated.

name: Magical Chicken & Parmesan Soup with Pappardelle
ingredients: 1 head of garlic, halved crosswise, plus 4 extra cloves, minced
2 carrots, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch cubes
1 Parmesan wedge, ~2 oz finely grated (1/2 cup), plus rind reserved
5 plum tomatoes, coarsely grated and skins discarded
1 small Fresno chile, seeded and finely chopped (1 tablespoon)
instructions: Put the chicken, halved head of garlic, onion, 8 1/2 cups water, and 2 1/4 teaspoons salt into a large saucepan. Bring to a boil, then lower to a simmer on medium-low, cover, and cook 80 minutes, or until the chicken is tender.
Remove the chicken and, when cool, shred the meat into large pieces, discarding skin, bones, and cartilage. Set aside.
Strain the stock through a sieve over a medium saucepan, discarding the solids. You'll need 7 cups; save any extra for another use.

Heat the oil in a large saucepan on medium-high. Add carrots and celery and cook 4 minutes until slightly softened. Add the minced garlic, bay leaves, thyme, and Parmesan rind and cook 30 seconds more. Add the grated tomatoes and tomato paste and cook 3 minutes, until the tomatoes cook down slightly. Pour in the 7 cups of stock, add 1/2 teaspoon salt and a generous grind of pepper, and bring to a boil. Lower to medium and cook 15 minutes, until the vegetables have softened.

Transfer 2-3 ladlefuls of soup (about 50/50 liquid to solids, avoiding the bay leaves and rind) to a blender, blitz until completely smooth, and stir back in. Add the pappardelle, shredded chicken, and a good grind of pepper and cook 15 minutes, until the pasta is al dente. Discard the bay leaves and Parmesan rind.
Divide among four bowls and top with basil and chile, a good grind of pepper, and a sprinkling of grated Parmesan.
notes: From Ottolenghi Test Kitchen. Swap the pappardelle for other pasta shapes (they also like mafalda). Leave out the chile for a kid-friendly version. Nutrition is estimated.

name: Asian Chicken Salad
ingredients: Dressing (mix in a screw-top jar and shake):
instructions: Make the dressing: combine all dressing ingredients in a screw-top jar and shake well.
Combine all salad ingredients in a large bowl.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss to coat.
notes: A crunchy, gingery main-dish salad. Nutrition is estimated.

name: Chicken with Two Vinegars
ingredients: 1 (3-4 lb) chicken, cut into serving pieces (boneless works)
1/4 cup Champagne vinegar or other white wine vinegar
1/2 cup French chardonnay or other white wine
6-8 plum tomatoes, peeled, seeded, and finely chopped
1/3 cup heavy cream (more if you like)
instructions: Season the chicken with salt and pepper. In a large cast-iron skillet, heat the oil and butter over medium-high heat and sauté the chicken until golden brown, 5-7 minutes a side. Transfer to a platter.

Pour off all but 1 tablespoon fat. Add the red and white vinegars and deglaze the pan, scraping the bottom. Add the shallots and sauté briefly until translucent. Add the white wine and bring to a boil. Return the chicken to the pan, cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer 15 minutes, then transfer chicken to a platter.

Add the chicken stock and tomatoes to the pan. Stir until the tomatoes break down into a sauce, about 10 minutes. Add the heavy cream, bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and return the chicken to the pan. Cook until heated through and coated with sauce, 5-10 minutes. Serve with steamed rice or a small tubular pasta like ziti, if desired.
notes: Shared by Maura (book club recipe, 2003). Nutrition is estimated.

name: White Chicken Chili
ingredients: 3 lbs bone-in, skin-on chicken breast halves, trimmed
Table salt and ground black pepper
3 medium poblano chiles, stemmed, seeded, and cut into large pieces
3 medium Anaheim chiles, stemmed, seeded, and cut into large pieces
2 medium onions, cut into large pieces (2 cups)
2 (16-oz) cans cannellini beans, drained and rinsed
4 scallions, white and light green parts sliced thin
instructions: Season chicken liberally with salt and pepper. Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high until just smoking. Add chicken skin-side down and cook without moving until skin is golden, about 4 minutes; turn and lightly brown the other side, about 2 minutes. Transfer to a plate; remove and discard skin.

While chicken browns, remove and discard ribs and seeds from 2 jalapeños; mince the flesh. In a food processor, process half the poblanos, Anaheims, and onions until the consistency of chunky salsa, 10-12 one-second pulses, scraping down halfway. Transfer to a bowl. Repeat with the remaining poblanos, Anaheims, and onions; combine with the first batch (don't wash the workbowl).

Pour off all but 1 tablespoon fat from the Dutch oven (add oil if needed) and reduce heat to medium. Add the minced jalapeños, chile-onion mixture, cumin, garlic, coriander, and 1/4 teaspoon salt. Cover and cook, stirring occasionally, until vegetables soften, about 10 minutes. Remove from heat.

Transfer 1 cup cooked vegetable mixture to the food processor. Add 1 cup beans and 1 cup broth and process until smooth, about 20 seconds. Add the vegetable-bean mixture, remaining 2 cups broth, and chicken breasts to the Dutch oven and bring to a boil over medium-high. Reduce to medium-low and simmer, covered, stirring occasionally, until chicken registers 160°F, 15-20 minutes.
Transfer chicken to a plate. Stir in the remaining beans and continue to simmer, uncovered, until beans are heated through and the chili has thickened slightly, about 10 minutes.

Mince the remaining jalapeño (reserve and mince ribs and seeds for extra heat). When chicken is cool enough to handle, shred into bite-sized pieces, discarding bones. Stir shredded chicken, lime juice, cilantro, scallions, and the remaining minced jalapeño into the chili and return to a simmer. Adjust seasonings with salt and pepper and serve.
notes: From Cook's Illustrated (Soups & Stews 2009) — "delicious!" Adjust heat with the minced jalapeño ribs and seeds in step 6. If Anaheims aren't available, add another poblano and jalapeño. Can substitute chicken thighs (increase cooking time in step 4 to ~40 min). Serve with sour cream, tortilla chips, and lime wedges. Nutrition is estimated.

name: Tuscan Bean & Italian Sausage Soup
ingredients: 4 cans (15 oz) cannellini beans, rinsed and drained
instructions: Brown the sausage; set aside.
In a 6-qt pot, brown the onion, garlic, celery, and mushrooms in the olive oil. Cook until tender.
Add the beans, broth, tomatoes, tomato paste, basil, oregano, salt, red pepper, sausage, and spinach. Boil until the spinach is wilted. Serve with Parmesan cheese, basil, and parsley.
notes: A hearty one-pot soup. Nutrition is estimated.

name: Lemon Posset
ingredients: Fresh berries or whipped cream, for serving (optional)
instructions: Combine the cream and sugar in a small saucepan over medium heat, stirring occasionally to dissolve the sugar. Once boiling, cook about 8-10 minutes until reduced to 2 cups, stirring occasionally and watching so it doesn't boil over (lift it off the heat briefly if it threatens to).
Off the heat, stir in the lemon juice. Let cool about 20 minutes; if a skin forms, strain or peel it off.

Pour into 6 small or medium ramekins. Refrigerate uncovered 3 hours until cold and fully set, then cover and keep up to 2 days. Let sit at room temperature 20 minutes before serving, with fresh berries or whipped cream if desired.
notes: The perfect blend of sweet and tart — a luxuriously creamy dessert from just three ingredients. Nutrition is estimated.

name: Green Pancakes with Lime Butter
ingredients: 8 tablespoons (1 stick) unsalted butter, at room temperature
instructions: Make the lime butter: put the butter in a medium bowl and beat with a wooden spoon until soft and creamy. Stir in the rest of the lime butter ingredients. Tip onto a sheet of plastic wrap and roll into a sausage shape, twisting the ends to seal. Chill until firm.
Wilt the spinach in a pan with a splash of water. Drain in a sieve and, when cool, squeeze hard to remove as much moisture as possible. Roughly chop and set aside.

Put the flour, whole egg, melted butter, salt, cumin, and milk in a large mixing bowl and whisk until smooth. Add the green onions, chiles, and spinach and mix with a fork. Whisk the egg white to soft peaks and gently fold it into the batter.

Pour a small amount of olive oil into a heavy frying pan over medium-high heat. For each pancake, ladle 2 tablespoons of batter into the pan and press down gently. You should get smallish pancakes, about 3 inches in diameter and 3/8 inch thick. Cook about 2 minutes on each side, until golden-green. Transfer to paper towels and keep warm. Continue, adding oil as needed, until the batter is used up.
To serve, pile up three warm pancakes per person and place a slice of the flavored butter on top to melt.
notes: From Ottolenghi (Plenty). A comforting brunch dish — serve with a salad of seasonal leaves, grilled halloumi, or smoked fish. Leftover lime butter keeps in the fridge; smear it over a baked sweet potato. Nutrition is estimated.

name: Zucchini & Goat Cheese Tart
ingredients: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
1 1/2 pounds zucchini, unpeeled and sliced 1/8 inch thick
8 ounces plain creamy goat cheese (such as Montrachet), at room temperature
instructions: Place the flour, 3/4 teaspoon salt, and the butter in a food processor and pulse 12 to 14 times, until the butter is the size of peas. With the processor running, pour the vinegar and ice water through the feed tube and process until the dough just comes together. Dump onto a floured board, form into a disk, wrap in plastic, and chill 30 minutes.

Meanwhile, place the zucchini in a colander set over a plate. Toss with 2 teaspoons salt and set aside 30 minutes. Spread the zucchini on a clean dish towel, roll it up, and squeeze gently to remove some liquid. Put into a bowl and toss with 1 tablespoon olive oil. With a fork, mash together the goat cheese, thyme, lemon zest, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon pepper; set aside.

Preheat oven to 400°F. Roll the dough out on a floured board to an 11-inch circle and place on a parchment-lined sheet pan. Spread with the goat cheese mixture, leaving a 1/2-inch border. Lay the zucchini in tightly overlapping circles, starting at the edge, until the tart is covered. Drizzle with the remaining tablespoon olive oil and sprinkle with pepper. Bake 40 to 50 minutes, until the dough is golden brown. Cut in wedges and serve hot, warm, or at room temperature.
notes: From Ina Garten (Cooking for Jeffrey). Shared by KBB. Salting and squeezing the zucchini keeps the tart from getting watery. Nutrition is estimated.

name: Jennifer Aniston Quinoa Salad
ingredients: 1/2 cup roasted and salted pistachios, chopped
1 (15 oz) can chickpeas, drained and rinsed
instructions: Rinse and drain the quinoa. Add quinoa and water to a small pot and bring to a boil. Reduce to a simmer, cover, and cook 15 minutes. Fluff with a fork and let cool 5-10 minutes.
In a large bowl, stir together the cooked quinoa, cucumber, parsley, mint, red onion, pistachios, chickpeas, lemon juice, olive oil, salt, pepper, and feta.
Serve immediately, or chill in the fridge a couple of hours before serving. Store in an airtight container up to 5 days.
notes: The viral Jennifer Aniston salad, made with quinoa (Eating Bird Food). Shared by KBB, who adds: a hard-boiled egg and/or avocado if on hand; roasted salted pepitas if no pistachios; the mint is key, and basil too; if you have preserved lemons, blend one into the dressing with the fresh lemon for more lemon intensity. Nutrition from the source recipe.

name: Vietnamese Beef Lettuce Wraps
ingredients: 2 teaspoons chili garlic sauce (or to taste)
Freshly ground black pepper, to taste
1 English cucumber, peeled, seeded, and chopped
3 scallions (white and light green parts), chopped
Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
1 ounce rice noodles, prepared per package and drained
instructions: Make the dipping sauce: combine all sauce ingredients in a small bowl and divide into small bowls for each diner.

Make the filling: heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Cook the onion and ginger 4-5 minutes until softened. Add the garlic and beef and cook 4-5 minutes until browned, crumbling into small pieces; drain well. Stir in the remaining filling ingredients, bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until nearly evaporated.
Make the cucumber relish: combine all relish ingredients in a bowl.
To assemble: spoon the meat mixture onto lettuce leaves and top with basil, carrots, rice noodles, peanuts, and lime wedges as desired. Roll up and serve with the dipping sauce.
notes: From A Chef's Kitchen (Carol). Shared by KBB, who says the basil and mint are key. Filling can be made 1-2 days ahead. Sodium runs high — use low-sodium soy sauce to cut it down. Nutrition from the source recipe (per serving).

name: Lemon Shallot Vinaigrette
ingredients: 1/4 cup fresh lemon juice (or substitute lime juice)
instructions: Peel and roughly chop the garlic and shallots. Add to a food processor and pulse until finely minced.
Add the honey, lemon juice, balsamic vinegar, red wine vinegar, salt, and pepper. Puree for a minute or two.
Add the oil and puree again. Taste and adjust seasoning and sweetness. Store in an airtight container up to a week in the fridge.
notes: From Rouxbe. Shared by KBB. A bright, tart everyday dressing with little oil — add a bit more oil if it's too tart for you. Great base to riff on (Dijon, fresh herbs, different vinegars). Nutrition is estimated.

name: One-Pan Farro with Tomatoes
ingredients: 9 ounces (about 1 1/2 cups) grape or cherry tomatoes, halved
1 1/4 teaspoons kosher or coarse sea salt
Up to 1/4 teaspoon red pepper flakes, to taste
1 tablespoon olive oil, plus extra for drizzling
A few basil leaves, cut into thin ribbons
instructions: Combine the water, farro, onion, garlic, tomatoes, salt, red pepper flakes, and 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large deep skillet.

Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to a gentle simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, for about 30 minutes, until the farro is cooked and the liquid has mostly evaporated. (Add a splash more water if it dries out before the farro is tender.)
Off the heat, drizzle with a little more olive oil, top with basil ribbons, and finish with grated Parmesan if desired.
notes: From Smitten Kitchen. Shared by KBB. A one-pan weeknight side — everything simmers together in a single skillet. Nutrition is estimated.

name: Pasta al Limone (Creamy Lemon Pasta)
ingredients: 16 ounces pasta, preferably spaghetti, linguine or bucatini (feel free to do half pasta, half zucchini noodles)
Kosher salt, to season the pasta water
2 tablespoons unrefined cold-pressed extra-virgin olive oil
Zest of 3 lemons + an extra for garnish, divided
4 tablespoons unsalted butter or plant butter
1 cup grated parmesan or pecorino cheese, divided
Fresh basil or mint leaves, for garnish
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Once it's at a raging boil, season with 1 tablespoon kosher salt.
Cook the pasta for 3 minutes less than the package recommends (e.g., if the package says 11 minutes, cook 8).
While the pasta cooks, heat the olive oil in a large saute pan or skillet over medium heat.
Add the zest of 2 lemons (eyeball it) to the pan along with half of the lemon juice, all of the butter, and season with black pepper and salt to taste.

Once the pasta is ready (not quite al dente yet), reserve 2 cups of the pasta cooking water, then drain the pasta and add it to the pan with the lemon mixture (or lift it out with tongs and leave the water in the pot).
Pour 1 1/3 cups pasta water into the pan and turn the heat to high. Cook the pasta in the skillet for 3 minutes - this releases starch that makes the dish creamy without cream.
For the first minute, stir constantly as the water evaporates.
Add the remaining lemon zest to the pan.
With 45 seconds to go, add the rest of the reserved lemon juice. Keep stirring on high heat until most, but not all, of the water has evaporated - you want some starchy liquid to remain.

When the pasta is perfectly al dente, remove from the heat and add 3/4 cup of the cheese. Toss until creamy and silky, adding extra pasta water as needed. Zest the last lemon on top and serve immediately with extra cheese on the side.
notes: From Pamela Salzman. No cream needed - starchy pasta water makes it silky. To turn it into a full meal, toss in plenty of fresh green vegetables (English peas, asparagus, broccoli, or Swiss chard) and a protein like sauteed shrimp or grilled chicken. A little fresh mint or basil brightens it further. Make it vegan with plant butter and vegan Parmesan. Nutrition is estimated for the base recipe.
